summary: Matched and linked all functions and data for `src/melee/it/items/itseakneedleheld.c`, turning the Sheik/Kirby held-needle item object from NonMatching to Matching. The PR replaced stubbed functions with typed implementations, added the held-needle item state table and item-var owner storage, updated headers/types, and corrected GALE01 split/symbol metadata. decomp.dev reported 6 new matches: `it_802B18B0`, `it_802B19AC`, `itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim`, plus `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2`.
what_changed: Implemented `it_802B18B0(Item_GObj*)`: validates the stored owner, chooses held-needle count with `ftSk_SpecialS_80111FA0` for Sheik or `ftKb_SpecialNSk_80106020` for Kirby, then iterates 6 needle JObjs and toggles `JOBJ_HIDDEN` with `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll`/`HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll`.

Implemented `it_802B19AC(Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*, Fighter_Part, ItemKind, float)`: builds a `SpawnItem`, zeroes velocity/damage/itcmd vars, sets parent links and facing, spawns with `Item_80268B18`, stores the parent fighter in `ip->xDD4_itemVar.seakneedleheld.owner`, and attaches the item to a fighter part with `Item_8026AB54`.

Implemented `itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim(Item_GObj*)`: handles missing or mismatched owner cases, checks Sheik/Kirby special-state helpers `ftSk_SpecialS_80111F70` and `ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105FF0`, updates visible needle count via `it_802B18B0`, and scales the needle JObj with `ftLib_800869D4(owner)`.
`itseakneedleheld.h` replaced unknown prototypes with concrete `Item_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `Fighter_Part`, and `ItemKind` signatures.
`itCharItems.h` added `itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars` containing `Fighter_GObj* owner`; `types.h` added the `seakneedleheld` member to `Item::xDD4_itemVar`.
`symbols.txt` converted the object-local `.sdata` strings and `.sdata2` float from global `it_804D5468`/`it_804D5470`/`it_804DCF88` names to local `@224`, `@225`, and `@186` labels.
`splits.txt` shortened the `itseakneedleheld.c` `.sdata` range from ending at `0x804D5478` to `0x804D5475` and `.sdata2` from ending at `0x804DCF90` to `0x804DCF8C`.
smart_moves: Added a dedicated held-needle item-var struct and union member instead of leaving owner storage implicit, making all owner checks and spawn initialization evidence-backed and type-safe.
Handled Sheik and Kirby copy versions in the same item code by switching on `ItemKind` and calling the matching `ftSk_*` or `ftKb_*` helper.
Matched the visible needle model behavior through HSD JObj hierarchy traversal: child/next traversal plus `JOBJ_HIDDEN` flags over a fixed 6-needle loop.
Preserved matching-sensitive compiler shape with `PAD_STACK`, explicit return labels/gotos in the anim function, a local block around the spawned `gobj`, and chained zero assignments.
Updated source, headers, item union, configure state, splits, and local symbol scopes together, which let both code and data for the object become matched/linked rather than only compiling the C.
decomp_lessons: Held character item objects may need an `xDD4_itemVar` struct that stores the owning `Fighter_GObj*`; validate both the stored item-var owner and `ip->owner` before using fighter-state data.

For character-specific item variants, inspect both the base fighter and Kirby-copy helper functions; the same item object can branch on `It_Kind_Seak_NeedleHeld` versus `It_Kind_Kirby_SeakNeedleHeld`.
When an item object has state callbacks, matching may require reconstructing the `ItemStateTable` in `.data`, not just replacing function stubs.

JObj visibility and scale operations can be central to small held-item logic; use `GET_JOBJ`, `HSD_JObjGetChild`, `HSD_JObjGetNext`, `HSD_JObjSetScale`, and hidden-flag APIs when the assembly walks model parts.
Data matching may require local `@NNN` symbols and exact split extents for `.sdata`/`.sdata2`; stale global `it_804D...` labels can prevent clean object-local data accounting.
Mark the object `Matching` in `configure.py` only after all relevant functions and data sections are matched; decomp.dev confirmed this PR reached 100% for the object entries it reported.
naming_conventions: Item variable typedef added as `itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars`; the corresponding `Item` union member is lowercase `seakneedleheld`.
Unrenamed address-based functions stayed in `it_802B18B0` / `it_802B19AC` form, while state callback names used the existing `itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim/Phys/Coll` pattern.

Header prototypes retain address comments such as `/* 2B18B0 */` and now use project typedefs (`Item_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `Fighter_Part`, `ItemKind`) instead of `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, `HSD_GObj*`, and raw `u32`.
Sheik/Kirby item kinds are distinguished with `It_Kind_Seak_NeedleHeld` and `It_Kind_Kirby_SeakNeedleHeld`.
Object-local constants in `symbols.txt` were named with `@224`, `@225`, and `@186` rather than exported `it_804D...` symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)` in `it_802B19AC` and `PAD_STACK(3 * 4)` in `itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim` were used to satisfy stack-layout/codegen constraints.
The anim function used explicit `goto ret_true` / `goto ret_false` labels to reproduce the matched control-flow shape.
Chained assignments zeroed `spawn.vel.x/y/z` and item command vars in the same expression form seen in the matched implementation.
A nested block around `Item_GObj* gobj = Item_80268B18(&spawn);` preserved local lifetime/order needed for the spawn helper match.
Split and symbol metadata were corrected alongside C code: `.sdata` and `.sdata2` ranges were tightened, and local data symbols replaced global labels.
review_feedback: The author disclosed being new to C/reverse decompilation and said they used Claude, Codex, Cursor, and assembly review to get small functions to 100%, expecting possible style/nuance cleanup.
ribbanya replied that nothing was too out of place, offered to clean it up for the author to study, and thanked them for the contribution.
No inline review comments or specific technical change requests were present in the slice.
decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code +860 bytes, linked code +944 bytes, total data -7 bytes, matched/linked data +33 bytes, and 6 new matches for the `itseakneedleheld` unit.
